ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-28-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steve Weinkauf for (petitioner) San Francisco Public Administrator.

The Court stated that there was no appearance at the last hearing, that there are a couple of issues and that it is not clear why the estate is paying expenses for property in New York and New Jersey. Mr. Weinkauf reported that it was found that the decedent owned these properties, that most of the time they were not rented, that they had to pay the taxes and mortgage and that they were sold. The Court stated that it was not inclined to grant the request to do a 6-year accounting, that it would prefer a two year accounting but would accept 2 3-year accountings. Mr. Weinkauf stated that they would like to get this completed as the sister is elderly and not well. The Court stated that the accounting petitions could come in at the same time but need to be broken down. The Court granted the petition for accounting and signed the order in open court.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-14-2015 Continued To Oct-28-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court stated that this is on for an amended first report and account, that the court has questions and that there is no one present to answer. The accounting petition was continued to October 28, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the hope that someone will appear to answer the questions.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Jun-12-2013 Continued To Jun-26-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar May-01-2013 Continued To Jun-12-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Weinkauf for San Francisco Public Administrator ("PA"). Sally Gratz for San Francisco Public Guardian.

Mr. Weinkauff reported that they are in the process of claiming escheated funds in the amount of $60,000.00, that the Public Administrator in New York is working on the New York property and that they are in the process of getting it appraised. In addition, Mr. Weinkauff also stated that the there are issues with squatters, that there is an asbestos problem and that the Jersey property sold. Ms. Gratz stated that the Highway 29 property was distributed half to Alvin and half into the Arnella's conservatorship, and that the conservatorship sold 100% interest. Counsel also stated that Cox and Co. were going to recontact Anthony Fleming. The Court raised the issue of Anthony receiving notice of today's hearing, stated that it was not sure if Anthony knows the PA's position, that he is to receive notice of the Status Report, responses to the Probate Examiner's notes and yesterday's response, expressed concern about the $52,000.00 distribution and stated that no more distributions are to be made at this point without Court order. Ms. Gratz explained that they are trying to keep Ms. Fleming at home, that the costs are about $10,000.00 to $15,000.00 a month, that they are down to about $21,000.00 in the conservatorship estate at this time which is why they are asking for another preliminary distribution, and that prior distribution was made because they had to do repairs that were required by Adult Protective Services. Ms. Gratz also stated that they are working with the PA, that they moved Ms. Fleming out of the home and then back in, that they still have a Case Manager, that attendant 24 hour care is $8,000.00 a month which is very reasonable and that Ms. Fleming has no other medical issues. Mr. Weinkauff stated that he could file a Petition to Determine Heirship. The status hearing was continued to June 12, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for notice to Anthony Fleming, for filing of the Petition for Heirship (which is to have the same hearing date of June 12, 2013) and for a subsequent declaration regarding need of a preliminary distribution setting out the financial needs of Ms. Fleming. Ms. Gratz is to see if they have enough funds to carry Ms. Flemings care until then.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Report On Status Of Administration, Petition For Preliminary Distribution And Petition For Instructions
Off Calendar Nov-30-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case number PES-07-289913 Estate of Henry Wilson Fleming.

Appearances: Sally Gratz for San Francisco Public Guardian (Conservator). Steven Weinkauf for San Francisco Public Administrator. Nancy Rasch for Arnelle Fleming

The Court approved the prelinary distribution of the Middleton property and $30,000.00 in cash (subject to receipt of a proposed order). The Court authorized the Public Administrator to sell the New York and the New Jersey properties. Status of filing of a Petition for Final Distribution on Henry Fleming's Estate was set for December 5, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Nuque.
